description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a slatted appearance. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the rough, speckled stone material that makes up the rest of the block. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, emphasizing the thickness and mass of the object. The stone surface has a mottled coloration, primarily gray with hints of brown and black, suggesting a natural or minimally processed stone. The overall form is geometric and precise, indicating it may serve a functional purpose such as drainage or as a barrier in an urban environment. The block's robust construction and lack of visible fasteners or joints imply it is designed to be heavy and stable.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on top and a rough, speckled surface. The grooves create a slatted pattern, contrasting with the solid, unadorned sides.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running from left to right.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stone's mottled texture visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooves' uniformity and the block's solid base.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view, again emphasizing the groove pattern and the rough stone surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a drainage or barrier element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears rough but intact, with no moss or chipping.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: flush with ground or pavement, likely aligned with other similar blocks
